core_intuitions: The softmax output of an LLM IS the parameter vector of a categorical distribution over the vocabulary; a probability and a distribution are the same object here.

Cross-entropy loss for a one-hot target collapses to a single number: -log q(x*), the model's surprise on the correct token; averaging it and exponentiating gives perplexity, an effective branching factor.
Cross-entropy decomposes as H(p,q)=H(p)+KL(p||q); since H(p) is constant in the model parameters, minimizing cross-entropy IS minimizing KL from data to model (this is the unified-picture claim).
KL divergence is asymmetric, and the asymmetry is behavioral: forward KL (MLE/training) is inclusive/mode-covering, reverse KL (RLHF/DPO penalty, variational inference) is exclusive/mode-seeking.
Entropy is expected surprisal: maximized by the uniform distribution, zero for a one-hot; it sets the floor of achievable cross-entropy.
Label smoothing puts a floor of eps/V on every class, keeping KL finite and preventing the correct-class logit from being pushed to infinity (overconfidence).

existing.name: prob-info-unified-picture
existing.issue: Renders correctly and legibly in both light and dark themes. The flow (data distribution -> CE loss with the H(p,q)=H(p)+KL decomposition -> model distribution -> perplexity branch and reverse-KL/RLHF branch) is exactly the chapter's meta-thesis and no box clips or overlaps. It handles the RELATIONSHIPS between concepts well, which is its job; it deliberately does not (and should not) also try to show what mode-covering vs mode-seeking looks like or what a categorical output looks like — those are the gaps the two new figures fill.
existing.improvement: Optional tiny polish only: in dark theme the two-line footer sits close to the horizontal divider; nudge it down ~6px for a touch more breathing room. Not required.

new_figures.name: softmax-categorical-crossentropy-anatomy
new_figures.concept: Anatomy of one training step: logits -> softmax categorical distribution over the vocabulary -> read the bar at the true token -> loss = -log q(x*); average and exponentiate to get perplexity.
new_figures.rationale: The chapter's opening thesis is that every forward pass reduces to 'compute a probability, then measure how far it is from what you wanted', and it repeatedly calls the categorical distribution 'the star of this textbook'. Yet nothing shows what that object looks like or how the one-hot target collapses the cross-entropy sum to the single term -log q(x*). This is the most load-bearing intuition in the chapter and it is currently only in prose and formulas. A single static frame makes 'the softmax IS a distribution' and 'the loss is just the surprise on the correct token' click, and grounds entropy and perplexity as properties of that same bar chart.
new_figures.placement: Cross-Entropy (right after the H(p_one-hot, q) = -log q(x*) derivation, before 'Why Cross-Entropy is THE LLM Loss')
new_figures.brief: A left-to-right pipeline across three linked panels over a tiny illustrative vocabulary of ~6 generic tokens (label them the, cat, sat, on, mat, dog). PANEL 1 'logits (any real numbers)': a horizontal or vertical bar chart of raw scores, mixing positive and negative bars (small integers like 3, 1, 0, -1, 2, -2), one baseline at zero so negativity is visible; caption 'unbounded scores from the network'. ARROW labeled 'softmax' (with tiny formula q_k = e^{z_k}/sum_j e^{z_j}) into PANEL 2 'categorical distribution q over the vocabulary (bars sum to 1)': the same 6 tokens now as probability bars all positive, varying heights, tallest bar on 'sat' which is the true token; annotate 'this vector IS a categorical distribution' and mark the total with a small 'Σ = 1'. Overlay the TARGET as a one-hot indicator on 'sat' — e.g. a thin outlined spike of height 1 or a star/caret above that bar, labeled 'one-hot target: all mass on the true token x*'. PANEL 3 'loss': draw a bracket/pointer from ONLY the 'sat' bar to the expression L = -log q(x*), and show the one-hot collapse explicitly: -Σ_k p_k log q_k  =  -log q(sat), with a note 'every other term is 0·log(...) = 0'. Include a small inset surprisal mapping so the reader feels the scale: a compact curve of -log q vs q on [0,1] going from ~0 at q=1 up steeply as q->0, with two dots — a tall bar (q near 1) -> small loss, a short bar (q near 0) -> large loss; label 'confident-and-right = little surprise; confident-and-wrong = big surprise'. FOOTER strip tying to the rest of the chapter: 'average -log q(x*) over all positions = cross-entropy loss; exp(that average) = perplexity = effective number of options the model is choosing among', plus 'entropy H(q) = expected surprisal = how wide these bars are (max when uniform, 0 when one-hot)'. Theme-safe: no hardcoded brand colors, use the palette tokens; keep all six tokens and numbers small illustrative integers; ensure bars, the one-hot overlay, and the inset all fit inside the viewBox with no overlap and a clear left-to-right reading order.

new_figures.name: kl-asymmetry-mode-covering-vs-seeking
new_figures.concept: Why forward and reverse KL behave differently: fitting a single-mode approximation q to a fixed two-mode target p. Forward KL (inclusive) spreads q to cover both modes; reverse KL (exclusive) locks q onto one mode.
new_figures.rationale: The chapter states forward KL is inclusive/mode-covering and reverse KL is exclusive/mode-seeking, and stresses this asymmetry is 'critical in variational inference and in RLHF/DPO'. The unified-picture figure only labels the two directions; it never shows the qualitatively different SHAPES they produce, which is the entire intuition and the classic reason the asymmetry matters. This single static two-panel figure is the canonical way to make 'asymmetric' visceral, and it directly explains the RLHF/DPO KL-penalty behavior the book returns to.
new_figures.placement: KL Divergence (immediately after the 'forward KL ... inclusive ... reverse KL ... exclusive' paragraph)
new_figures.brief: Two side-by-side panels sharing an identical FIXED bimodal target p(x): a filled/low-opacity density with two clearly separated humps of similar height and a valley between them, drawn the same in both panels and labeled 'target p (two modes)'. In each panel overlay ONE single-mode approximation q(x) (the family can only represent a single hump), drawn as a distinct outlined curve. LEFT panel title 'Forward KL  D_KL(p || q)  — inclusive / mode-covering': q is broad and flattened, straddling BOTH humps with its peak sitting in the valley between them; annotate 'q must put mass wherever p has mass' and pin the driving reason to the valley region: 'penalty blows up where p>0 but q≈0  →  q is forced to spread and cover everything (even the empty valley)'. RIGHT panel title 'Reverse KL  D_KL(q || p)  — exclusive / mode-seeking': q is narrow and sits squarely on ONE of the two humps, ignoring the other; annotate 'q avoids regions where p≈0' and pin the reason to the abandoned second mode and the valley: 'penalty blows up where q>0 but p≈0  →  q retreats onto a single mode'. Keep the two q curves visually distinguishable from p (e.g. p filled, q a bold stroke) and legible in both themes via palette tokens, no hardcoded colors. FOOTER band spanning both panels connecting to the chapter: 'MLE / cross-entropy training minimizes forward KL(p_data || q_theta) → the model must cover all modes of the data.  RLHF / DPO adds a reverse-KL(pi || pi_ref) penalty → the policy stays concentrated near the reference model.' Everything inside the viewBox; axes can be minimal (just an x-axis baseline); no numeric benchmarks, this is a schematic of shapes.

summary: The chapter is concept-rich and, apart from one strong meta-relationship diagram, figure-poor on the two most foundational intuitions. Keep the existing unified-picture figure (correct and legible in both themes; it does the relationship-mapping job well). Add exactly two static figures: (1) the softmax->categorical->cross-entropy anatomy, which grounds the chapter's central claim that the LLM output IS a categorical distribution and the loss is just -log q(x*) surprise (with perplexity and entropy read off the same bars); and (2) the KL-asymmetry mode-covering vs mode-seeking two-panel, which makes the inclusive/exclusive distinction visceral and explains the forward-KL-training vs reverse-KL-RLHF contrast the book keeps invoking. No animation is warranted — both are single-frame concepts. This keeps the chapter to 3 total figures, disciplined against a figure-per-section sprawl.
